name: Marie Short House
key_features: long narrow planning for cross ventilation
junction_lessons: separate lightweight wall systems from ground moisture
express environmental control devices as part of the architecture
use thin, readable edge conditions
why_relevant: A core precedent for lightweight Australian residential construction, passive climate response, and expressed material systems.

name: Magney House
key_features: roof form driven by climate and water collection
junction_lessons: make roof geometry work with drainage logic
allow environmental performance to shape edge conditions
keep eaves and roof lines clean but purposeful
why_relevant: Very useful for roof edge detailing, rainwater logic, and making environmental performance part of architectural expression.

name: Simpson-Lee House
key_features: careful response to exposed site conditions
junction_lessons: small edge decisions strongly affect overall architectural clarity
protect openings in exposed conditions
resolve lightweight elements with precision rather than bulk
why_relevant: Useful for refining lightweight detailing in exposed conditions and for disciplined edge-making.

name: Ball-Eastaway House
junction_lessons: lift delicate structures away from damp ground conditions
express support points clearly
keep enclosure systems light and readable
why_relevant: Helpful for thinking about elevated construction, lightweight frames, and delicate enclosure systems.

name: Marika-Alderton House
key_features: shade and air movement as primary design drivers
junction_lessons: design enclosure as adjustable rather than fixed
prioritise shade and airflow at every edge

why_relevant: Highly relevant for climate-led detailing, operable screens, and lightweight tropical construction.

name: Bingie Farm
key_features: environmental performance embedded in simple detailing
junction_lessons: robust details can still be elegant
let environmental logic drive repeated components

name: Lovett Bay House
key_features: simple but highly resolved domestic construction

let movement joints be deliberate rather than hidden
use projection, clearance, and drip logic instead of relying on sealants

name: Palm Garden House
junction_lessons: use offset and layering to clarify transitions
make thresholds read as intentional architectural moments

why_relevant: Useful for expressed frame logic, threshold articulation, and refined residential material transitions.

name: Invisible House
junction_lessons: reduce visual noise at key interfaces
use continuity and shadow to soften transitions
let the building sit into its site rather than dominate it
why_relevant: Helpful for quiet, refined transitions and integrating architectural detail with site conditions.

name: Springwater House
junction_lessons: design edge conditions as filters rather than barriers
keep roof and threshold details visually light

name: Basin Beach House
junction_lessons: balance exposure with protection
choose robust fixings and edges in coastal conditions
let transitions between screen and enclosure stay legible

name: North Avoca House
junction_lessons: treat the outer skin as a defensive layer

name: Permanent Camping 2
junction_lessons: a small number of materials can produce strong detail coherence
keep assemblies legible and repeatable

name: Two Sheds

name: Birchgrove House
junction_lessons: tight urban conditions benefit from disciplined layering

name: Kew House
junction_lessons: make flashings and datum lines work architecturally

junction_lessons: privacy layers can be tectonically legible
